compiled_state.title: As a developer, recipe tasks with missing engines degrade gracefully to LLM sub-agent tasks
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: When an engine is not installed (as detected by S4's pre-flight), recipe tasks gracefully fall back to LLM sub-agent dispatch with a ticket comment recording the degradation.
**Why**: Recipe infrastructure should enhance the sprint, not block it. Missing engines should degrade gracefully rather than failing.
**Scope**:
- IN: Consume S4's missing-engines list (not re-check independently), construct LLM sub-agent task from recipe task spec, translate recipe intent to natural-language task description using registry's capability_description field, ticket comment recording fallback with engine name
- OUT: Engine installation, pre-flight detection (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a recipe task whose engine is missing becomes an LLM sub-agent task with equivalent intent
  ← Satisfies: "recipe tasks gracefully degrade to LLM sub-agent tasks"
- When this story is complete, the fallback LLM task has a natural-language intent description derived from the recipe's capability_description field in the registry, not raw executor flags or parameters
  ← Satisfies: LLM-comprehensible fallback (adversarial review finding)
- When this story is complete, a ticket comment records the fallback with the engine name and reason
  ← Satisfies: "with a ticket comment recording the fallback"
- When this story is complete, the fallback LLM task produces identical downstream structure (review, commit, verify) to normal sub-agent tasks
  ← Satisfies: pipeline compatibility
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] LLM fallback tasks must count toward usage if dso-t7af (throttling) is active

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
